revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (en:Abe clan); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-25) == {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start|1= {{WikiProject Military history|class=Start|B1=n|B2=n|B3=y|B4=y|B5=n|Japanese=yes}} {{WikiProject Japan|importance=Mid|history=y|milhist=y|ainu=y}} }} ==Assessment== The additional info looks great. If you can add just one reference then I would say make the change to "Start" class. There is enough info to upgrade but there should just be at least one source. --[[User:Looper5920|Looper5920]] 23:29, 4 October 2006 (UTC) ::I did actually intend to add those while I was working on the article. Thanks for pointing it out. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 09:06, 5 October 2006 (UTC) A well written article. But I don't think there is any evidence that the Ainu/Emishi Abes of Northern Japan are related to the Abes "descended from a son of Emperor Kōgen" from Mie Prefecture. The Northern Abes originated in the Appi River basin in northern Iwate in the 9th century and may have taken their name from the river. Since a number of Abes served as shoguns and governors of Mutsu there may have been political bonds between them and the Abes may have granted permission to the Emishi to use the name. Perhaps the Northern Abes should be listedunder "Other Abe Families". —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:Iwatebud|Iwatebud]] ([[User talk:Iwatebud|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/Iwatebud|contribs]]) 01:53, 13 September 2007 (UTC) ::I wasn't aware of any Abe family descended from Ainu/Emishi. This article focuses on the Wajin (Yamato) clan. If you have information on a different clan, descended from Emishi of the Appi River valley, please do create an article on them. It sounds interesting. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 07:40, 13 September 2007 (UTC) ---- There is a problem here. The "Chinjufu shogun" designation was a temporary position given to a major clan leader, typically military, after the middle Heian. It was not a permanent position. The Abe were given a title called "Superintendant of the Aborigines." Actually, the Minamoto were given the "Chinjufu shogun" designation only after the Abe refused to pay taxes to the central government. I will edit this part. —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:K.H.|K.H.]] ([[User talk:K.H.|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/K.H.|contribs]]) 08:30, 13 December 2007 (UTC) Another problem. The Abe were never appointed to the position of "Chinjufu shogun." —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:K.H.|K.H.]] ([[User talk:K.H.|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/K.H.|contribs]]) 09:08, 13 December 2007 (UTC) == External links modified == Hello fellow Wikipedians, I have just modified one external link on [[Abe clan]]. Please take a moment to review [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?diff=prev&oldid=787410316 my edit]. If you have any questions, or need the bot to ignore the links, or the page altogether, please visit [[User:Cyberpower678/FaQs#InternetArchiveBot|this simple FaQ]] for additional information. I made the following changes: *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20080411113817/http://www.uni-tuebingen.de/geschichte-japans/manabu/shoshidai.htm to http://www.uni-tuebingen.de/geschichte-japans/manabu/shoshidai.htm When you have finished reviewing my changes, you may follow the instructions on the template below to fix any issues with the URLs. {{sourcecheck|checked=false|needhelp=}} Cheers.—[[User:InternetArchiveBot|'''InternetArchiveBot''']] ([[User talk:InternetArchiveBot|Report bug]]) 07:11, 25 June 2017 (UTC) == Name meaning? == Is there a known meaning or suggested etymologies for "Abe"? — [[User:Imeriki al-Shimoni|al-Shimoni]] ([[User talk:Imeriki al-Shimoni|talk]]) 18:11, 7 December 2023 (UTC)

revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (en:Abe clan); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 18:41, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-25) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 18:41, 25 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start|1= {{WikiProject Military history|class=Start|B1=n|B2=n|B3=y|B4=y|B5=n|Japanese=yes}} {{WikiProject Japan|importance=Mid|history=y|milhist=y|ainu=y}} }} ==Assessment== The additional info looks great. If you can add just one reference then I would say make the change to "Start" class. There is enough info to upgrade but there should just be at least one source. --[[User:Looper5920|Looper5920]] 23:29, 4 October 2006 (UTC) ::I did actually intend to add those while I was working on the article. Thanks for pointing it out. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 09:06, 5 October 2006 (UTC) A well written article. But I don't think there is any evidence that the Ainu/Emishi Abes of Northern Japan are related to the Abes "descended from a son of Emperor Kōgen" from Mie Prefecture. The Northern Abes originated in the Appi River basin in northern Iwate in the 9th century and may have taken their name from the river. Since a number of Abes served as shoguns and governors of Mutsu there may have been political bonds between them and the Abes may have granted permission to the Emishi to use the name. Perhaps the Northern Abes should be listedunder "Other Abe Families". —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:Iwatebud|Iwatebud]] ([[User talk:Iwatebud|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/Iwatebud|contribs]]) 01:53, 13 September 2007 (UTC) ::I wasn't aware of any Abe family descended from Ainu/Emishi. This article focuses on the Wajin (Yamato) clan. If you have information on a different clan, descended from Emishi of the Appi River valley, please do create an article on them. It sounds interesting. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 07:40, 13 September 2007 (UTC) ---- There is a problem here. The "Chinjufu shogun" designation was a temporary position given to a major clan leader, typically military, after the middle Heian. It was not a permanent position. The Abe were given a title called "Superintendant of the Aborigines." Actually, the Minamoto were given the "Chinjufu shogun" designation only after the Abe refused to pay taxes to the central government. I will edit this part. —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:K.H.|K.H.]] ([[User talk:K.H.|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/K.H.|contribs]]) 08:30, 13 December 2007 (UTC) Another problem. The Abe were never appointed to the position of "Chinjufu shogun." —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:K.H.|K.H.]] ([[User talk:K.H.|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/K.H.|contribs]]) 09:08, 13 December 2007 (UTC) == External links modified == Hello fellow Wikipedians, I have just modified one external link on [[Abe clan]]. Please take a moment to review [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?diff=prev&oldid=787410316 my edit]. If you have any questions, or need the bot to ignore the links, or the page altogether, please visit [[User:Cyberpower678/FaQs#InternetArchiveBot|this simple FaQ]] for additional information. I made the following changes: *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20080411113817/http://www.uni-tuebingen.de/geschichte-japans/manabu/shoshidai.htm to http://www.uni-tuebingen.de/geschichte-japans/manabu/shoshidai.htm When you have finished reviewing my changes, you may follow the instructions on the template below to fix any issues with the URLs. {{sourcecheck|checked=false|needhelp=}} Cheers.—[[User:InternetArchiveBot|'''InternetArchiveBot''']] ([[User talk:InternetArchiveBot|Report bug]]) 07:11, 25 June 2017 (UTC) == Name meaning? == Is there a known meaning or suggested etymologies for "Abe"? — [[User:Imeriki al-Shimoni|al-Shimoni]] ([[User talk:Imeriki al-Shimoni|talk]]) 18:11, 7 December 2023 (UTC)

revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (en:Abe clan); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:03, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 18:41, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-25, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1241804527 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:03, 25 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start|1= {{WikiProject Military history|class=Start|B1=n|B2=n|B3=y|B4=y|B5=n|Japanese=yes}} {{WikiProject Japan|importance=Mid|history=y|milhist=y|ainu=y}} }} ==Assessment== The additional info looks great. If you can add just one reference then I would say make the change to "Start" class. There is enough info to upgrade but there should just be at least one source. --[[User:Looper5920|Looper5920]] 23:29, 4 October 2006 (UTC) ::I did actually intend to add those while I was working on the article. Thanks for pointing it out. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 09:06, 5 October 2006 (UTC) A well written article. But I don't think there is any evidence that the Ainu/Emishi Abes of Northern Japan are related to the Abes "descended from a son of Emperor Kōgen" from Mie Prefecture. The Northern Abes originated in the Appi River basin in northern Iwate in the 9th century and may have taken their name from the river. Since a number of Abes served as shoguns and governors of Mutsu there may have been political bonds between them and the Abes may have granted permission to the Emishi to use the name. Perhaps the Northern Abes should be listedunder "Other Abe Families". —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:Iwatebud|Iwatebud]] ([[User talk:Iwatebud|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/Iwatebud|contribs]]) 01:53, 13 September 2007 (UTC) ::I wasn't aware of any Abe family descended from Ainu/Emishi. This article focuses on the Wajin (Yamato) clan. If you have information on a different clan, descended from Emishi of the Appi River valley, please do create an article on them. It sounds interesting. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 07:40, 13 September 2007 (UTC) ---- There is a problem here. The "Chinjufu shogun" designation was a temporary position given to a major clan leader, typically military, after the middle Heian. It was not a permanent position. The Abe were given a title called "Superintendant of the Aborigines." Actually, the Minamoto were given the "Chinjufu shogun" designation only after the Abe refused to pay taxes to the central government. I will edit this part. —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:K.H.|K.H.]] ([[User talk:K.H.|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/K.H.|contribs]]) 08:30, 13 December 2007 (UTC) Another problem. The Abe were never appointed to the position of "Chinjufu shogun." —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:K.H.|K.H.]] ([[User talk:K.H.|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/K.H.|contribs]]) 09:08, 13 December 2007 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:03, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == External links modified == Hello fellow Wikipedians, I have just modified one external link on [[Abe clan]]. Please take a moment to review [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?diff=prev&oldid=787410316 my edit]. If you have any questions, or need the bot to ignore the links, or the page altogether, please visit [[User:Cyberpower678/FaQs#InternetArchiveBot|this simple FaQ]] for additional information. I made the following changes: *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20080411113817/http://www.uni-tuebingen.de/geschichte-japans/manabu/shoshidai.htm to http://www.uni-tuebingen.de/geschichte-japans/manabu/shoshidai.htm When you have finished reviewing my changes, you may follow the instructions on the template below to fix any issues with the URLs. {{sourcecheck|checked=false|needhelp=}} Cheers.—[[User:InternetArchiveBot|'''InternetArchiveBot''']] ([[User talk:InternetArchiveBot|Report bug]]) 07:11, 25 June 2017 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:03, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Name meaning? == Is there a known meaning or suggested etymologies for "Abe"? — [[User:Imeriki al-Shimoni|al-Shimoni]] ([[User talk:Imeriki al-Shimoni|talk]]) 18:11, 7 December 2023 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:03, 25 February 2026 (UTC)

revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (en:Abe clan); add local discussion section + dated import note

If you can add just one reference then I would say make the change to "Start" class. There is enough info to upgrade but there should just be at least one source. --[[User:Looper5920|Looper5920]] 23:29, 4 October 2006 (UTC) ::I did actually intend to add those while I was working on the article. Thanks for pointing it out. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 09:06, 5 October 2006 (UTC) A well written article. But I don't think there is any evidence that the Ainu/Emishi Abes of Northern Japan are related to the Abes "descended from a son of Emperor Kōgen" from Mie Prefecture. The Northern Abes originated in the Appi River basin in northern Iwate in the 9th century and may have taken their name from the river. Since a number of Abes served as shoguns and governors of Mutsu there may have been political bonds between them and the Abes may have granted permission to the Emishi to use the name. Perhaps the Northern Abes should be listedunder "Other Abe Families". —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:Iwatebud|Iwatebud]] ([[User talk:Iwatebud|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/Iwatebud|contribs]]) 01:53, 13 September 2007 (UTC) ::I wasn't aware of any Abe family descended from Ainu/Emishi. This article focuses on the Wajin (Yamato) clan. If you have information on a different clan, descended from Emishi of the Appi River valley, please do create an article on them. It sounds interesting. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 07:40, 13 September 2007 (UTC) ---- There is a problem here. The "Chinjufu shogun" designation was a temporary position given to a major clan leader, typically military, after the middle Heian. It was not a permanent position. The Abe were given a title called "Superintendant of the Aborigines." Actually, the Minamoto were given the "Chinjufu shogun" designation only after the Abe refused to pay taxes to the central government. I will edit this part. —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:K.H.|K.H.]] ([[User talk:K.H.|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/K.H.|contribs]]) 08:30, 13 December 2007 (UTC) Another problem. The Abe were never appointed to the position of "Chinjufu shogun." —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:K.H.|K.H.]] ([[User talk:K.H.|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/K.H.|contribs]]) 09:08, 13 December 2007 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:38, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == External links modified == Hello fellow Wikipedians, I have just modified one external link on [[Abe clan]]. If you have any questions, or need the bot to ignore the links, or the page altogether, please visit [[User:Cyberpower678/FaQs#InternetArchiveBot|this simple FaQ]] for additional information. revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (en:Abe clan); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:09, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:38, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:03, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 18:41, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-25, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1241804527 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:09, 25 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start|1= {{WikiProject Military history|class=Start|B1=n|B2=n|B3=y|B4=y|B5=n|Japanese=yes}} {{WikiProject Japan|importance=Mid|history=y|milhist=y|ainu=y}} }} ==Assessment== The additional info looks great. If you can add just one reference then I would say make the change to "Start" class. There is enough info to upgrade but there should just be at least one source. --[[User:Looper5920|Looper5920]] 23:29, 4 October 2006 (UTC) ::I did actually intend to add those while I was working on the article. Thanks for pointing it out. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 09:06, 5 October 2006 (UTC) A well written article. But I don't think there is any evidence that the Ainu/Emishi Abes of Northern Japan are related to the Abes "descended from a son of Emperor Kōgen" from Mie Prefecture. The Northern Abes originated in the Appi River basin in northern Iwate in the 9th century and may have taken their name from the river. Since a number of Abes served as shoguns and governors of Mutsu there may have been political bonds between them and the Abes may have granted permission to the Emishi to use the name. Perhaps the Northern Abes should be listedunder "Other Abe Families". —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:Iwatebud|Iwatebud]] ([[User talk:Iwatebud|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/Iwatebud|contribs]]) 01:53, 13 September 2007 (UTC) ::I wasn't aware of any Abe family descended from Ainu/Emishi. This article focuses on the Wajin (Yamato) clan. If you have information on a different clan, descended from Emishi of the Appi River valley, please do create an article on them. It sounds interesting. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 07:40, 13 September 2007 (UTC) ---- There is a problem here. The "Chinjufu shogun" designation was a temporary position given to a major clan leader, typically military, after the middle Heian. It was not a permanent position. The Abe were given a title called "Superintendant of the Aborigines." Actually, the Minamoto were given the "Chinjufu shogun" designation only after the Abe refused to pay taxes to the central government. I will edit this part. —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:K.H.|K.H.]] ([[User talk:K.H.|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/K.H.|contribs]]) 08:30, 13 December 2007 (UTC) Another problem. The Abe were never appointed to the position of "Chinjufu shogun." —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:K.H.|K.H.]] ([[User talk:K.H.|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/K.H.|contribs]]) 09:08, 13 December 2007 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:09, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == External links modified == Hello fellow Wikipedians, I have just modified one external link on [[Abe clan]]. revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (en:Abe clan); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:26, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:09, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:38, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:03, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 18:41, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-25, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1241804527 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:26, 25 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start|1= {{WikiProject Military history|class=Start|B1=n|B2=n|B3=y|B4=y|B5=n|Japanese=yes}} {{WikiProject Japan|importance=Mid|history=y|milhist=y|ainu=y}} }} ==Assessment== The additional info looks great. If you can add just one reference then I would say make the change to "Start" class. There is enough info to upgrade but there should just be at least one source. --[[User:Looper5920|Looper5920]] 23:29, 4 October 2006 (UTC) ::I did actually intend to add those while I was working on the article. Thanks for pointing it out. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 09:06, 5 October 2006 (UTC) A well written article. But I don't think there is any evidence that the Ainu/Emishi Abes of Northern Japan are related to the Abes "descended from a son of Emperor Kōgen" from Mie Prefecture. The Northern Abes originated in the Appi River basin in northern Iwate in the 9th century and may have taken their name from the river. Since a number of Abes served as shoguns and governors of Mutsu there may have been political bonds between them and the Abes may have granted permission to the Emishi to use the name. Perhaps the Northern Abes should be listedunder "Other Abe Families". —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:Iwatebud|Iwatebud]] ([[User talk:Iwatebud|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/Iwatebud|contribs]]) 01:53, 13 September 2007 (UTC) ::I wasn't aware of any Abe family descended from Ainu/Emishi. This article focuses on the Wajin (Yamato) clan. If you have information on a different clan, descended from Emishi of the Appi River valley, please do create an article on them. It sounds interesting. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 07:40, 13 September 2007 (UTC) ---- There is a problem here. The "Chinjufu shogun" designation was a temporary position given to a major clan leader, typically military, after the middle Heian. It was not a permanent position. The Abe were given a title called "Superintendant of the Aborigines." Actually, the Minamoto were given the "Chinjufu shogun" designation only after the Abe refused to pay taxes to the central government. I will edit this part. —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:K.H.|K.H.]] ([[User talk:K.H.|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/K.H.|contribs]]) 08:30, 13 December 2007 (UTC) Another problem. The Abe were never appointed to the position of "Chinjufu shogun." —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:K.H.|K.H.]] ([[User talk:K.H.|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/K.H.|contribs]]) 09:08, 13 December 2007 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:26, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == External links modified == Hello fellow Wikipedians, I have just modified one external link on [[Abe clan]]. Please take a moment to review [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?diff=prev&oldid=787410316 my edit]. If you have any questions, or need the bot to ignore the links, or the page altogether, please visit [[User:Cyberpower678/FaQs#InternetArchiveBot|this simple FaQ]] for additional information. I made the following changes: *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20080411113817/http://www.uni-tuebingen.de/geschichte-japans/manabu/shoshidai.htm to http://www.uni-tuebingen.de/geschichte-japans/manabu/shoshidai.htm When you have finished reviewing my changes, you may follow the instructions on the template below to fix any issues with the URLs. {{sourcecheck|checked=false|needhelp=}} Cheers.—[[User:InternetArchiveBot|'''InternetArchiveBot''']] ([[User talk:InternetArchiveBot|Report bug]]) 07:11, 25 June 2017 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:26, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Name meaning? == Is there a known meaning or suggested etymologies for "Abe"? — [[User:Imeriki al-Shimoni|al-Shimoni]] ([[User talk:Imeriki al-Shimoni|talk]]) 18:11, 7 December 2023 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:26, 25 February 2026 (UTC)

revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (en:Abe clan); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:26, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:26, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:09, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:38, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:03, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 18:41, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-25, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1241804527 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:26, 25 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start|1= {{WikiProject Military history|class=Start|B1=n|B2=n|B3=y|B4=y|B5=n|Japanese=yes}} {{WikiProject Japan|importance=Mid|history=y|milhist=y|ainu=y}} }} ==Assessment== The additional info looks great. If you can add just one reference then I would say make the change to "Start" class. There is enough info to upgrade but there should just be at least one source. --[[User:Looper5920|Looper5920]] 23:29, 4 October 2006 (UTC) ::I did actually intend to add those while I was working on the article. Thanks for pointing it out. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 09:06, 5 October 2006 (UTC) A well written article. But I don't think there is any evidence that the Ainu/Emishi Abes of Northern Japan are related to the Abes "descended from a son of Emperor Kōgen" from Mie Prefecture. The Northern Abes originated in the Appi River basin in northern Iwate in the 9th century and may have taken their name from the river. Since a number of Abes served as shoguns and governors of Mutsu there may have been political bonds between them and the Abes may have granted permission to the Emishi to use the name. Perhaps the Northern Abes should be listedunder "Other Abe Families". —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:Iwatebud|Iwatebud]] ([[User talk:Iwatebud|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/Iwatebud|contribs]]) 01:53, 13 September 2007 (UTC) ::I wasn't aware of any Abe family descended from Ainu/Emishi. This article focuses on the Wajin (Yamato) clan. If you have information on a different clan, descended from Emishi of the Appi River valley, please do create an article on them. It sounds interesting. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 07:40, 13 September 2007 (UTC) ---- There is a problem here. The "Chinjufu shogun" designation was a temporary position given to a major clan leader, typically military, after the middle Heian. It was not a permanent position. The Abe were given a title called "Superintendant of the Aborigines." Actually, the Minamoto were given the "Chinjufu shogun" designation only after the Abe refused to pay taxes to the central government. I will edit this part. —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:K.H.|K.H.]] ([[User talk:K.H.|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/K.H.|contribs]]) 08:30, 13 December 2007 (UTC) Another problem. The Abe were never appointed to the position of "Chinjufu shogun." —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:K.H.|K.H.]] ([[User talk:K.H.|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/K.H.|contribs]]) 09:08, 13 December 2007 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:26, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == External links modified == Hello fellow Wikipedians, I have just modified one external link on [[Abe clan]]. Please take a moment to review [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?diff=prev&oldid=787410316 my edit]. If you have any questions, or need the bot to ignore the links, or the page altogether, please visit [[User:Cyberpower678/FaQs#InternetArchiveBot|this simple FaQ]] for additional information. I made the following changes: *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20080411113817/http://www.uni-tuebingen.de/geschichte-japans/manabu/shoshidai.htm to http://www.uni-tuebingen.de/geschichte-japans/manabu/shoshidai.htm When you have finished reviewing my changes, you may follow the instructions on the template below to fix any issues with the URLs. {{sourcecheck|checked=false|needhelp=}} Cheers.—[[User:InternetArchiveBot|'''InternetArchiveBot''']] ([[User talk:InternetArchiveBot|Report bug]]) 07:11, 25 June 2017 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:26, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Name meaning? == Is there a known meaning or suggested etymologies for "Abe"? — [[User:Imeriki al-Shimoni|al-Shimoni]] ([[User talk:Imeriki al-Shimoni|talk]]) 18:11, 7 December 2023 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:26, 25 February 2026 (UTC)

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] previous contributors were [[User:en>Plumber]], [[User:Immanuelle]], see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Abe_clan here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:26, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:26, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:09, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:38, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:03, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 18:41, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-25, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1241804527 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:26, 25 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start|1= {{WikiProject Military history|class=Start|B1=n|B2=n|B3=y|B4=y|B5=n|Japanese=yes}} {{WikiProject Japan|importance=Mid|history=y|milhist=y|ainu=y}} }} ==Assessment== The additional info looks great. If you can add just one reference then I would say make the change to "Start" class. There is enough info to upgrade but there should just be at least one source. --[[User:Looper5920|Looper5920]] 23:29, 4 October 2006 (UTC) ::I did actually intend to add those while I was working on the article. Thanks for pointing it out. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 09:06, 5 October 2006 (UTC) A well written article. But I don't think there is any evidence that the Ainu/Emishi Abes of Northern Japan are related to the Abes "descended from a son of Emperor Kōgen" from Mie Prefecture. The Northern Abes originated in the Appi River basin in northern Iwate in the 9th century and may have taken their name from the river. Since a number of Abes served as shoguns and governors of Mutsu there may have been political bonds between them and the Abes may have granted permission to the Emishi to use the name. Perhaps the Northern Abes should be listedunder "Other Abe Families". —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:Iwatebud|Iwatebud]] ([[User talk:Iwatebud|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/Iwatebud|contribs]]) 01:53, 13 September 2007 (UTC) ::I wasn't aware of any Abe family descended from Ainu/Emishi. This article focuses on the Wajin (Yamato) clan. If you have information on a different clan, descended from Emishi of the Appi River valley, please do create an article on them. It sounds interesting. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 07:40, 13 September 2007 (UTC) ---- There is a problem here. The "Chinjufu shogun" designation was a temporary position given to a major clan leader, typically military, after the middle Heian. It was not a permanent position. The Abe were given a title called "Superintendant of the Aborigines." Actually, the Minamoto were given the "Chinjufu shogun" designation only after the Abe refused to pay taxes to the central government. I will edit this part. —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:K.H.|K.H.]] ([[User talk:K.H.|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/K.H.|contribs]]) 08:30, 13 December 2007 (UTC) Another problem. The Abe were never appointed to the position of "Chinjufu shogun." —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:K.H.|K.H.]] ([[User talk:K.H.|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/K.H.|contribs]]) 09:08, 13 December 2007 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:26, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == External links modified == Hello fellow Wikipedians, I have just modified one external link on [[Abe clan]]. Please take a moment to review [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?diff=prev&oldid=787410316 my edit]. If you have any questions, or need the bot to ignore the links, or the page altogether, please visit [[User:Cyberpower678/FaQs#InternetArchiveBot|this simple FaQ]] for additional information. I made the following changes: *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20080411113817/http://www.uni-tuebingen.de/geschichte-japans/manabu/shoshidai.htm to http://www.uni-tuebingen.de/geschichte-japans/manabu/shoshidai.htm When you have finished reviewing my changes, you may follow the instructions on the template below to fix any issues with the URLs. {{sourcecheck|checked=false|needhelp=}} Cheers.—[[User:InternetArchiveBot|'''InternetArchiveBot''']] ([[User talk:InternetArchiveBot|Report bug]]) 07:11, 25 June 2017 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:26, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Name meaning? == Is there a known meaning or suggested etymologies for "Abe"? — [[User:Imeriki al-Shimoni|al-Shimoni]] ([[User talk:Imeriki al-Shimoni|talk]]) 18:11, 7 December 2023 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:26, 25 February 2026 (UTC)

revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:26, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:26, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:09, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:38, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:03, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 18:41, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-25, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1241804527 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:26, 25 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start|1= {{WikiProject Military history|class=Start|B1=n|B2=n|B3=y|B4=y|B5=n|Japanese=yes}} {{WikiProject Japan|importance=Mid|history=y|milhist=y|ainu=y}} }} ==Assessment== The additional info looks great. If you can add just one reference then I would say make the change to "Start" class. There is enough info to upgrade but there should just be at least one source. --[[User:Looper5920|Looper5920]] 23:29, 4 October 2006 (UTC) ::I did actually intend to add those while I was working on the article. Thanks for pointing it out. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 09:06, 5 October 2006 (UTC) A well written article. But I don't think there is any evidence that the Ainu/Emishi Abes of Northern Japan are related to the Abes "descended from a son of Emperor Kōgen" from Mie Prefecture. The Northern Abes originated in the Appi River basin in northern Iwate in the 9th century and may have taken their name from the river. Since a number of Abes served as shoguns and governors of Mutsu there may have been political bonds between them and the Abes may have granted permission to the Emishi to use the name. Perhaps the Northern Abes should be listedunder "Other Abe Families". —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:Iwatebud|Iwatebud]] ([[User talk:Iwatebud|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/Iwatebud|contribs]]) 01:53, 13 September 2007 (UTC) ::I wasn't aware of any Abe family descended from Ainu/Emishi. This article focuses on the Wajin (Yamato) clan. If you have information on a different clan, descended from Emishi of the Appi River valley, please do create an article on them. It sounds interesting. [[User:LordAmeth|LordAmeth]] 07:40, 13 September 2007 (UTC) ---- There is a problem here. The "Chinjufu shogun" designation was a temporary position given to a major clan leader, typically military, after the middle Heian. It was not a permanent position. The Abe were given a title called "Superintendant of the Aborigines." Actually, the Minamoto were given the "Chinjufu shogun" designation only after the Abe refused to pay taxes to the central government. I will edit this part. —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:K.H.|K.H.]] ([[User talk:K.H.|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/K.H.|contribs]]) 08:30, 13 December 2007 (UTC) Another problem. The Abe were never appointed to the position of "Chinjufu shogun." —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[User:K.H.|K.H.]] ([[User talk:K.H.|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/K.H.|contribs]]) 09:08, 13 December 2007 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:26, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == External links modified == Hello fellow Wikipedians, I have just modified one external link on [[Abe clan]]. I made the following changes: *Added archive https://web.archive.org/web/20080411113817/http://www.uni-tuebingen.de/geschichte-japans/manabu/shoshidai.htm to http://www.uni-tuebingen.de/geschichte-japans/manabu/shoshidai.htm When you have finished reviewing my changes, you may follow the instructions on the template below to fix any issues with the URLs. {{sourcecheck|checked=false|needhelp=}} Cheers.—[[User:InternetArchiveBot|'''InternetArchiveBot''']] ([[User talk:InternetArchiveBot|Report bug]]) 07:11, 25 June 2017 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:26, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Name meaning? == Is there a known meaning or suggested etymologies for "Abe"? — [[User:Imeriki al-Shimoni|al-Shimoni]] ([[User talk:Imeriki al-Shimoni|talk]]) 18:11, 7 December 2023 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:26, 25 February 2026 (UTC)
